Only summarize Agent2’s reasoning about what causes stimulation of the respiratory center — no extra commentary or explanation beyond that point.

The main physiological trigger for stimulating the respiratory center is an increase in arterial CO₂, which lowers cerebral spinal fluid pH by forming carbonic acid, thereby activating central chemoreceptors in the medulla. This results in heightened ventilation to counteract respiratory acidosis.
